title: Smoke-Cold Irish Linguine Pasta
description: A kid-friendly, tangy Irish dish featuring linguine pasta, smoked cold ingredients, and a cheese pull. Perfect for a weeknight dinner, this recipe is easy to make and fun to eat.

ingredients.name: Smoked cream cheese
ingredients.notes: Softened, cold-pressed option for best results.

instructions: Bring a large kazán or cauldron of salted water to a rolling boil over high heat.
Cook the linguine pasta according to package instructions until al dente, about 8 minutes.
Drain the pasta and set it aside.
In the same kazán, melt the salted butter over medium heat.
Add the chopped scallions and cook for 2-3 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the softened smoked cream cheese until melted and smooth.
Add the shredded Cotswold cheddar cheese and stir until melted and a cheese pull is achieved.
Add the cooked linguine pasta to the kazán and toss with the cheese sauce until combined.
Top with smoked salmon fillet, chopped fresh dill, and serve immediately.
